reforms.description: The Icelandic government has established the New Business Venture Fund to provide start-up capital and invest in early-stage and expanding companies, addressing the financing challenges faced by innovative start-ups. This fund aims to enhance access to capital for new ventures and strengthen the economy through innovation-oriented projects, particularly in light of the reduced availability of venture capital since the dot-com bubble burst in 2001.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to improve financing for innovative start-ups, which is expected to enhance their growth potential and contribute positively to the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The New Business Venture Fund has been established and is operational, providing necessary capital to start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the fund represents a significant but not structural change in the support for start-ups, enhancing existing mechanisms rather than creating a new system.
reforms.source_quote: The government has proposed that some of the proceeds from the sale of Iceland Telecom go to a new investment fund –with pension funds and financial enterprises also invited to contribute– which would be directed towards firms in their early expansion phase.
